outcomes.change_description: Retire this node as a duplicate of [8e027adda45d]. Both nodes point to the same government URL and describe the same regulation with near-identical content. Remap all citation edges from this node to [8e027adda45d]. Mark as deprecated/duplicate.

outcomes.change_description: Reduce confidence from 0.97 to 0.82. Add caveat to description: 'Quantitative LCA claims (91% reduction in environmental impact, 92% lower GHG vs conventional HA) are drawn from a Givaudan-commissioned, non-independently-audited lifecycle assessment and should be treated as manufacturer-reported figures pending third-party verification.' Factual launch details (product name, date, venue, Bacillus strain, 50 kDa MW specification) remain well-supported.

outcomes.change_description: Reduce confidence from 0.92 to 0.65. Remove the 'BC market valued at ~USD 668M in 2024, projected to reach USD 2.2B by 2034' market figures unless a primary market research citation (e.g., Grand View Research, Allied Market Research) is added. Replace 'drop-in biodegradable replacement' with 'actively commercialized biodegradable candidate requiring formulation adaptation to address hydrophilicity, electrolyte sensitivity, and optical clarity gaps versus polyacrylates.' Retain BIOWEG Series A milestone, 50,000L fermentation achievement, and MicBeads/RheoWeg product references as well-sourced.

outcomes.node_label: γ-PGA Bacillus Fermentation: Super-Humectant for Cosmetics
outcomes.challenge_summary: Reduce confidence from 0.90 to 0.75. Remove 'Super-Humectant' from node title and description; replace with 'high-performance humectant with skin barrier and film-forming properties.' Qualify the TEWL
outcomes.change_description: Reduce confidence from 0.90 to 0.75. Remove 'Super-Humectant' from node title and description; replace with 'high-performance humectant with skin barrier and film-forming properties.' Qualify the TEWL/HA comparison as: 'TEWL reduction comparable to hyaluronic acid is claimed by commercial suppliers and supported by in-vitro data; independent peer-reviewed clinical comparison citation required to confirm.' Retain yield data (101.1 g/L B. subtilis ZJU-7), INCI listing, and commercial supplier information as well-supported elements.

outcomes.node_label: AMSilk/Givaudan Recombinant Spider Silk for Hair and Skin

outcomes.change_description: Reduce confidence from 0.88 to 0.68. Remove '>50,000L capacity' bioreactor specification; replace with 'industrial-scale bioreactor fermentation' pending a primary source (AMSilk/Givaudan technical documentation or peer-reviewed bioprocess paper). Add clarification on post-2019 commercial status: 'Current commercial availability of AMSilk/Givaudan spider silk ingredients in cosmetics at production scale not publicly confirmed as of 2024-2025.' Retain INCI listing Sr-Spider Polypeptide-4, Givaudan acquisition date, and application descriptions.

outcomes.node_label: Spider Silk Cosmetics ScienceDirect Review 2025
Animal-Free Chondroitin Sulfate E via Engineered E. coli
outcomes.challenge_summary: Correct the regulatory statement. Replace 'CS has existing FDA food ingredient approval' with: 'Chondroitin sulfate is established as a dietary supplement ingredient in the United States under DSHEA;
outcomes.change_description: Correct the regulatory statement. Replace 'CS has existing FDA food ingredient approval' with: 'Chondroitin sulfate is established as a dietary supplement ingredient in the United States under DSHEA; it does not hold FDA food additive approval or GRAS status for food use. This eliminates supply-chain dependency on animal-derived cartilage for cosmetic and supplement applications.' No confidence reduction required for the scientific content, which remains well-sourced via the Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology 2024 paper.

outcomes.change_description: Reduce confidence from 0.88 to 0.72. Revise FDA GRAS language to: 'Marinova Pty Ltd fucoidan products rely on self-affirmed GRAS determination; no FDA-reviewed GRAS Notice (GRN) with a public response letter is confirmed in the FDA GRAS Notices database as of 2025.' Revise EU Novel Foods language to: 'Fucoidan-sourced products have received EU Novel Foods authorization under Regulation (EU) 2015/2283; specific company, product, and authorization number should be referenced from the EU Novel Food Catalogue for verification.' Retain all functional cosmetic claims (anti-aging, soothing, TRL 8-9 for established species) as these are not challenged.

outcomes.node_label: EU 2023/2055 forces reformulation of acrylate-based cosmetics by 2027-2035
outcomes.challenge_summary: Confidence remains at 0.45 (already capped as unsourced quantitative). Revise description to add: 'Note: whether crosslinked polyacrylic acids (carbomers) and acrylate crosspolymers meet the SPM defin
outcomes.change_description: Confidence remains at 0.45 (already capped as unsourced quantitative). Revise description to add: 'Note: whether crosslinked polyacrylic acids (carbomers) and acrylate crosspolymers meet the SPM definition under EU 2023/2055 remains under active regulatory interpretation; ECHA technical guidance on swellable/dispersible polymer test exemptions may affect scope.' Change 'forces reformulation' to 'is likely to require reformulation pending final regulatory interpretation of carbomer and acrylate copolymer SPM classification.'

outcomes.node_label: Microplastics in Cosmetics PMC Review 2024
outcomes.challenge_summary: Correct description metadata only; no confidence reduction warranted for the scientific content. Change 'EU 2024 ban on intentionally added microplastics' to 'EU October 2023 restriction on intentiona
outcomes.change_description: Correct description metadata only; no confidence reduction warranted for the scientific content. Change 'EU 2024 ban on intentionally added microplastics' to 'EU October 2023 restriction on intentionally added microplastics (Regulation EU 2023/2055, entered into force October 17, 2023).' This corrects the date attribution without affecting the review's scientific validity.

outcomes.node_label: C16 Biosciences achieved 50,000L industrial-scale fermentation for cosmetic oils
outcomes.challenge_summary: Reduce confidence from 0.87 to 0.72. Add caveat to description: '250x land use efficiency figure is derived from C16 Biosciences own analysis and has not been independently verified via third-party LC
outcomes.change_description: Reduce confidence from 0.87 to 0.72. Add caveat to description: '250x land use efficiency figure is derived from C16 Biosciences own analysis and has not been independently verified via third-party LCA, consistent with the epistemic standard applied to manufacturer-reported lifecycle claims in this graph. Dozens of beauty brand partnerships is unverified marketing language.' Retain 50,000L January 2024 fermentation milestone as company-reported operational fact with appropriate attribution.

outcomes.node_label: C16 Biosciences Palmless Fermentation Platform
Arcaea $78M Series A: synthetic biology platform for petrochemical-free beauty
outcomes.challenge_summary: Reduce confidence from 0.84 to 0.68. Remove the sentence 'The Ginkgo-Amyris collaboration achieved only 11% commercialization rate across 70+ pipeline ingredients, signaling high attrition in synbio b
outcomes.change_description: Reduce confidence from 0.84 to 0.68. Remove the sentence 'The Ginkgo-Amyris collaboration achieved only 11% commercialization rate across 70+ pipeline ingredients, signaling high attrition in synbio beauty' from the description pending identification of a primary post-2023 source. The 2021 SynBioBeta source predates Amyris bankruptcy by two years and cannot contain retrospective pipeline attrition analysis. All Arcaea-specific facts (funding amount, backers, platform, ScentARC, TRL range) should be retained as well-supported. If a primary post-2023 source for the Ginkgo-Amyris figure is identified, create a dedicated source node and re-link at separately assessed confidence.

outcomes.change_description: Reduce confidence from 0.82 to 0.60. Revise description to: (a) qualify 113-118% proliferation figure as 'reported in primary studies cited by PMC10672628; primary source, levan species, cell model, and concentration require disambiguation before this figure can be treated as established'; (b) qualify TEWL equivalence with HA as 'claimed in supplier literature and supported by limited in-vitro data; independent peer-reviewed clinical comparison has not been confirmed, consistent with the standard applied to gamma-PGA in this graph.' Flag as needs_source=true for the proliferation primary study. Retain film-forming, moisturizing, and commercial status content.

outcomes.node_label: Marine/Non-Marine Bacterial EPS Skincare Review (PMC 2023)
Hyaluronic acid fermentation market — HA at TRL 9, global capacity up 28%
outcomes.challenge_summary: Reduce confidence from 0.86 to 0.40. Revise description to clarify: the $10.73B figure refers to the total global HA market across all sectors (pharma, orthopedic, ophthalmic, nutraceuticals, cosmetic
outcomes.change_description: Reduce confidence from 0.86 to 0.40. Revise description to clarify: the $10.73B figure refers to the total global HA market across all sectors (pharma, orthopedic, ophthalmic, nutraceuticals, cosmetics combined), not the fermentation-derived cosmetic HA sub-market, which is approximately $63.7M in 2025 per [7d15419a20d9] — approximately 160x smaller. Add caveat: '28% capacity increase (2022-2024) and $1.2B Asia-Pacific investment figures are estimates without dedicated market intelligence citations; treat as directional indicators pending primary source attribution.' Flag '2025 Bacillus GRAS validation' as requiring a specific 2025 publication source. Retain TRL 9 designation for fermentation-derived HA and cross-reference Givaudan launch details to [97e5747b1db0].

outcomes.change_description: Reduce confidence from 0.87 to 0.60. Remove 'Opal Biotech Biogums Series, 2024' from description pending identification of a dedicated post-2023 industry/supplier source node; if a 2024 source is identified, create a dedicated source node and restore with proper attribution. Revise LMW-WG glycerol/adaptive evolution claim to: 'Low-MW welan gum produced via glycerol-fed adaptive evolution of Sphingomonas ATCC 31555 shows novel texture properties [primary research study cited in PMC10672628; primary study requires disambiguation for direct attribution].' Retain CP Kelco gellan leadership, deacylated variants, diutan gum suspension stability content as well-supported.

outcomes.node_label: Marine/Non-Marine Bacterial EPS Skincare Review (PMC 2023)
EU Green Claims Directive 2024/825 tightens bio-based cosmetic claims
outcomes.challenge_summary: Reduce confidence from 0.84 to 0.72. Add caveat: 'NATRUE-specific operational details (75% of brand range certification requirement, prohibition on GMO ingredients) are attributed to Credence Research
outcomes.change_description: Reduce confidence from 0.84 to 0.72. Add caveat: 'NATRUE-specific operational details (75% of brand range certification requirement, prohibition on GMO ingredients) are attributed to Credence Research market report — an institutionally inappropriate primary source for certification rules. These details require the NATRUE Standard document (current version, natrue.org) as authoritative primary source. The GMO prohibition claim is especially material as it may affect eligibility of precision-fermentation-derived polymers produced via engineered organisms for NATRUE certification; this implication should be verified directly against the NATRUE Standard before citing at high confidence.' Flag as needs_source=true for NATRUE Standard document; recommend creating a dedicated source node for natrue.org primary certification standards. EU Directive 2024/825 factual core and COSMOS market statistics are retained without reduction.

outcomes.node_label: Marine bacterial EPS (extremophile-derived) commercialized as cosmetic actives, not rheology modifiers
outcomes.challenge_summary: Reduce confidence from 0.87 to 0.78. Revise 'NOT as bulk rheology modifiers' to: 'currently not commercially deployed as bulk rheology modifiers — reflecting economic positioning constraints (lower yi
outcomes.change_description: Reduce confidence from 0.87 to 0.78. Revise 'NOT as bulk rheology modifiers' to: 'currently not commercially deployed as bulk rheology modifiers — reflecting economic positioning constraints (lower yields and higher production costs relative to established mesophilic EPS producers) and commercial positioning choices, rather than any intrinsic structural incompatibility with rheological function.' Qualify TRL 3-5 as: 'TRL 3-5 is a researcher-applied assessment based on the research maturity stages described in PMC10672628; formal TRL designation is not explicitly assigned in the cited source and should be treated as an analytical inference from reported laboratory/pilot research status, not a directly cited quantification.' Retain core finding that commercially deployed extremophile EPS function as bioactive actives and that deep-sea thermophile/psychrophile EPS production remains early-stage.

outcomes.node_label: Extremophile-Derived Bioactives in Cosmeceuticals (Life 2025, MDPI)
BIOWEG Series A Bacterial Cellulose Scale-Up 2024
outcomes.challenge_summary: Reduce confidence from 0.97 to 0.80. Add caveat to description clarifying: (1) source is trade press coverage based substantially on company press release; (2) industrial-scale production of MicBeads
outcomes.change_description: Reduce confidence from 0.97 to 0.80. Add caveat to description clarifying: (1) source is trade press coverage based substantially on company press release; (2) industrial-scale production of MicBeads and RheoWeg had not commenced at time of reporting; (3) 'precision fermentation' reflects the source article's own terminology — technically, Komagataeibacter xylinus BC fermentation is standard submerged fermentation, not engineered-organism precision fermentation. Core business facts (€16M Series A, Germany first industrial BC plant, product names, EU microplastic context) remain well-attested and are not reduced. Performance claims require independent peer-reviewed validation.
